name: Rahsaan Roland Kirk
abstract: Rahsaan Roland Kirk (born Ronald Theodore Kirk; August 7, 1935 – December 5, 1977), known earlier in his career simply as Roland Kirk, was an American jazz multi-instrumentalist who played tenor saxophone, flute, and many other instruments. He was renowned for his onstage vitality; in his shows, virtuoso improvisation was accompanied by …

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Turré
about_quotes.evidence: Rahsaan I first played with when I was 18 in San Francisco... once I came to New York I played with him again up until he passed.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Turré interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 29, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Richard Wyands
about_quotes.evidence: Well I worked with him a few times and I did a record with him. “We Free Kings” I believe it’s called.
about_quotes.doc_title: Richard Wyands interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Terri Lyne Carrington
about_quotes.evidence: Rahsaan Roland Kirk was like one of the first people that I played with.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terri Lyne Carrington interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...Rahsaan Roland Kirk killing on it. ... [INTERVIEWEE] That was at Rudy Van Gelder’s. I remember that. I do remember that most of the guys were there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: My greatest support in this discussion was Rahsaan, whom I liked tremendously. Rahsaan was a terrific person.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Griffin
about_quotes.evidence: So I did all of his orchestrating or writing for all of the Atlantic records that he did.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Griffin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 12, 2024
